root_text.ud8.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud8.1:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā bhikkhū nibbānapaṭisaṁyuttāya dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti.
root_text.ud8.1:1.4: Tedha bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.ud8.1:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud8.1:3.1: “Atthi, bhikkhave, tadāyatanaṁ, yattha neva pathavī, na āpo, na tejo, na vāyo, na ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ, na viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ, na ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ, na nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ, nāyaṁ loko, na paraloko, na ubho candimasūriyā.
root_text.ud8.1:3.2: Tatrāpāhaṁ, bhikkhave, neva āgatiṁ vadāmi, na gatiṁ, na ṭhitiṁ, na cutiṁ, na upapattiṁ;

translation_text.ud8.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud8.1:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants with a Dhamma talk about extinguishment.
translation_text.ud8.1:1.4: And those mendicants were paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.ud8.1:2.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud8.1:3.1: “There is, mendicants, that dimension where there is no earth, no water, no fire, no wind; no dimension of infinite space, no dimension of infinite consciousness, no dimension of nothingness, no dimension of neither perception nor non-perception; no this world, no other world, no moon or sun.
translation_text.ud8.1:3.2: There, mendicants, I say there is no coming or going or remaining or passing away or reappearing.
translation_text.ud8.1:3.3: It is not established, does not proceed, and has no support.
translation_text.ud8.1:3.4: Just this is the end of suffering.”

variant_text.ud8.1:1.4: Tedha bhikkhū → te ca bhikkhū (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1) | aṭṭhiṁ katvā → aṭṭhī katvā (bj); aṭṭhikatvā (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1) | sabbaṁ cetaso → sabbaṁ cetasā (itipi aññasuttesu)
